terminology.note: vector, embedding, cluster, nearest neighbour, cosine similarity, dimension all used consistently with 6.3-6.9. 'Embedding' matches 6.7 (words->vectors, related meanings->similar vectors). 'Cosine similarity' matches 6.9 (angle between meaning-vectors, length-independent), correctly cited as the similarity score in the search panel. 'Nearest neighbour' matches 6.5 (neighbourhood / closest point) and 6.8 (nearest_neighbour_answer). 'Cluster' is newly named here as the topic's own concept (cluster of word-vectors) and is grounded as 'clump of nearby dots = similar embeddings', consistent with 6.5 distance-as-similarity. No synonym drift.

prereq_ordering.note: manifest prerequisites [6.3, 6.5, 6.6, 6.7, 6.8, 6.9] all precede 6.10 in sequence; matches the corpus Prerequisites section exactly. No forward references. PCA and t-SNE are name-and-deferred ('named buttons that do the squashing', internals 'covered later'), explicitly NOT taught, and are tagged 'PCA/t-SNE (deferred)' in cross_refs rather than listed as prerequisites. Correct handling.

arc_completeness.note: Lands the week's arc. Explicitly synthesises the 6.1-6.9 build ('meaning can be written as numbers') and delivers the payoff: turning the abstract 'word as a point in space' (6.5) into something visible/rotatable. Learning Objectives cover the tool, the squashing rationale, reading clusters, search/nearest-neighbour, and rank-by-hand-then-verify. No uncovered facet for a single-topic capstone.

no_duplication.note: Single-topic module, so no intra-module pair to compare. Prior concepts (vector, embedding, dot product, cosine similarity, nearest neighbour) are reused-and-cited, not re-taught: each carries a back-reference (6.3/6.5/6.6/6.7/6.8/6.9) and the corpus states 'those terms... will not be re-defined - only used.' Only genuinely new material (Projector, squashing-to-view, cluster reading, search UI) is taught.

lab_alignment.note: Matches lab_activity_seed for week 6 on every clause: TensorFlow Embedding Projector (corpus 5.1, Implementation, reading In Practice); watch word clusters form in 2D/3D; rank 5 pre-computed sentence vectors by hand then verify in the browser (Hands-On Exercise steps 1-4); no coding (stated in Introduction, 5.5 scope boundary, Key Takeaways); Journal Entry #3 (explicitly named); Assessment 2 due this week (noted in exercise; manifest next_assessment A2 due_week 6).
